# Constrained trail-pct sweep — walkfold + portfolio-simulator dual constraint

**Read-only research. No spec changes shipped.** Generated by
`.claude/local_scripts/constrained_trail_sweep.py` on 2026-05-14.

## Why this report exists

The 2026-05-14 walk-fold validation (`trail_pct_walkfold_validation.md`)
showed `trail_pct=0.10` dominating the deployed `0.30` on Sharpe + Max DD
in 5/6 non-overlapping folds. On that basis I started the
`feat-trail-pct-tighten-to-0.10` ship workstream: persisted a new
full-window backtest with `trail_pct=0.10`, repointed
`spec_config.PHASE1B_WINNER_RUN_ID`, and regenerated `active_spec.json`.

The regenerated spec exposed a problem the walkfold validation did not:
the spec's own `backtest_expectations.portfolio_max_dd_pct` came back at
**-37.0%**, exceeding the live `risk.max_account_drawdown_pct = 0.30`
catastrophic guard. Under a regime resembling Jun-Jul 2025 (which
produced this -37%), the engine would trip its kill switch and halt new
entries.

The walkfold "Max DD" was per-fold sum-of-per-trade-fractions cumulative-
P&L drawdown — no capital constraint, no compounding. The portfolio
simulator (`crypto/execution/backtest/report.py:simulate_portfolio`)
models the actual deployment shape: $1k starting capital, max 6
concurrent positions, deploy_fraction=0.8, leverage=1.0, compounding
position size off current equity. **These are different metrics and
they disagree on the ordering.**

This sweep tests every trail value through both lenses.

## Method

For each `trail_pct ∈ {0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30}`:

1. Persist (idempotently) a full-window backtest. Same axes as deployed
   (`horizon=10d, policy=D, selection=top_n/n=6, activation_pct=0.01`),
   same window (`2025-04-05 → 2026-05-07`), post-parabolic filter ON
   (matching live behaviour), walkfold OOS predictions as the data
   source. Persisted via `H.run_backtest(... dry_run=False)` →
   `crypto_backtest_runs` / `_summary` / `_trades`.
2. Read `sharpe_ratio` and `max_drawdown_pct` from `crypto_backtest_summary`.
3. Run `simulate_portfolio(starting_capital=1000, max_positions=6,
   deploy_fraction=0.8, leverage=1.0)` — exactly the same call the
   spec's `_backtest_expectations` makes.
4. Reuse walkfold dominance count from the prior validation.

Decision rule (per operator instructions):

- Walkfold dominance over `trail=0.30` in **≥ 4 of 6 folds**, AND
- Portfolio simulator `max_dd ≤ -25%` (5pp margin under the -30% live kill switch), AND
- Portfolio simulator Sharpe meaningfully above the 0.30 baseline (+0.10).

Ship the lowest trail satisfying all three. Else HOLD.

## Results

| trail | run_id (new) | harness sharpe | harness max_dd | **portfolio sharpe** | **portfolio max_dd** | portfolio ann. return | walkfold dom. vs 0.30 | walkfold mean sharpe |
|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 0.10 | `backtest_10d_D_top_n_2cdb9a5d` | 7.15 | -14.82% | **4.98** | **-37.03%** | +4771% | 5/6 | 7.42 |
| 0.15 | `backtest_10d_D_top_n_4197da6c` | 6.88 | -15.39% | **4.78** | **-37.67%** | +3421% | 5/6 | 6.92 |
| 0.20 | `backtest_10d_D_top_n_bbff4a2b` | 6.68 | -15.91% | **4.62** | **-38.31%** | +2625% | 4/6 | 6.71 |
| 0.25 | `backtest_10d_D_top_n_beb5f882` | 6.53 | -17.38% | **4.67** | **-31.60%** | +2639% | 3/6 | 6.52 |
| 0.30 | `backtest_10d_D_top_n_f74ee424` | 6.32 | -16.98% | **4.88** | **-31.91%** | +2566% | 0/6 (baseline) | 6.35 |

## Constraint check

Kill switch = -30%. Safety floor = -25%. Walkfold floor = 4/6.

| trail | walkfold ≥4/6 | portfolio_max_dd ≤ -25% | portfolio_sharpe > 0.30+0.10 | OVERALL |
|---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 0.10 | ✓ | ✗ (-37.0%) | ✓ (+0.10) | **HOLD** |
| 0.15 | ✓ | ✗ (-37.7%) | ✗ (-0.10) | **HOLD** |
| 0.20 | ✓ | ✗ (-38.3%) | ✗ (-0.26) | **HOLD** |
| 0.25 | ✗ | ✗ (-31.6%) | ✗ (-0.21) | **HOLD** |
| 0.30 | — | ✗ (-31.9%) | — | baseline |

**No trail value passes.** Every single value tested produces
portfolio-level max_drawdown deeper than the -25% safety floor, AND
deeper than the -30% live kill switch.

## Decision: HOLD deployed `trail_pct = 0.30`

Walkfold dominance is real but does not survive translation to the
capital-constrained, compounding portfolio simulation that models live
deployment. The operational risk (kill-switch trips during F2-like
regimes) outweighs the per-fold gains.

## Methodological finding (for ADR consumption)

**Walkfold per-fold dominance is not sufficient for shipping decisions
on parameters that affect portfolio drawdown.** The walkfold metric
sums per-trade net P&L within each fold, producing a "cumulative-equity-
curve max drawdown" that ignores capital constraints, position-size
caps, and compounding. The portfolio simulator
(`simulate_portfolio`) models all three faithfully — and disagrees on
the metric that matters most for live deployment, the catastrophic
guard.

Future spec changes affecting Policy D parameters that govern exit
timing (trail_pct, activation_pct), position concurrency (top_n,
max_concurrent), or any axis where compounding amplifies tail risk
must validate against the portfolio simulator AND against the live
`risk.max_account_drawdown_pct` envelope, not just the walkfold raw
P&L.

A useful additional report would be: **per-fold portfolio_max_dd**, not
per-fold raw P&L drawdown. The walkfold validation script could be
extended to run `simulate_portfolio` on each fold's trades separately
— that would have caught this issue before the ship workstream
started.

## Adjacent finding: filter-ON vs filter-OFF inconsistency

The deployed spec's `portfolio_max_dd_pct = -23.7%` came from
`backtest_10d_D_top_n_a02e15a0`, which has parameters
`{"policy_params":{"trail_pct":0.3},"selection_params":{"n":6}}` — no
`apply_postparabolic_filter` field, meaning the run was performed
with the filter **OFF**.

But live execution (`crypto/exports/write_daily_predictions.py`)
applies the post-parabolic filter to every prediction batch. So:

- Deployed spec's portfolio_max_dd: -23.7% (filter OFF)
- This sweep's trail=0.30 portfolio_max_dd: -31.9% (filter ON)

The spec's published expectations therefore **understate** real
deployment drawdown by ~8 percentage points. The divergence-alert
threshold of 20% is wide enough to absorb this for now, but the
underlying inconsistency should be tracked: the spec's
backtest_expectations should reflect what the engine will actually
do, not a no-filter ancestor.

Resolving this would mean re-pointing `PHASE1B_WINNER_RUN_ID` to
`backtest_10d_D_top_n_f74ee424` (the filter-ON trail=0.30 row just
persisted) — same trail, but matched-to-live filter status. That alone
would update the spec's `portfolio_max_dd_pct` from -23.7% to -31.9%,
which **also exceeds** the -30% kill switch. Worth a separate session.

## What was persisted to the DB (audit trail)

5 new rows in `crypto_backtest_runs` / `_summary` / `_trades`:

```
backtest_10d_D_top_n_2cdb9a5d   trail=0.10  (this session, kept for audit)
backtest_10d_D_top_n_4197da6c   trail=0.15  (this session)
backtest_10d_D_top_n_bbff4a2b   trail=0.20  (this session)
backtest_10d_D_top_n_beb5f882   trail=0.25  (this session)
backtest_10d_D_top_n_f74ee424   trail=0.30  (this session, filter-ON 0.30 baseline)
```

All filter-ON, full-window 2025-04-05 → 2026-05-07. Operator can
re-query these without re-running. Per the operator's instruction
("Don't revert spec rows yet; keep the branch alive while we extend
the analysis"), these are retained.

- 5 new backtest rows persisted to the DB (listed above). No other DB writes.
- Branch `feat-trail-pct-tighten-to-0.10` remains alive locally (no commits), per operator instruction.
